claim_status: ADR-0009 decision-review template only; no Servo source strategy, no source baseline, no feature profile, no owner-selected option, no dependency approval, no component approval, no JavaScript-runtime change, no source import, no source adoption, no compatibility claim, no performance claim, no security claim, no support commitment, no release-code authorization, no PB-002 readiness promotion, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not name placeholder options, title-only reviewers, self-approval, private contact details, implied source adoption, or inferred owner authority; a real decision review must replace null fields with owner-reviewed evidence.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-001 source identity and checkout trust
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Owner-selected source baseline, signed-tag or equivalent provenance decision, accepted source equivalence policy, blob and legal review, and rerun plan.
Owner-accepted replay protocol, clean-target reproduction on an independent host or clean VM, target/cache isolation replay, and retained success/failure logs.
Owner-selected SBOM format/toolchain, full SBOM, advisory snapshot, duplicate-version decisions, and generated/native inclusion rules.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-004 license notice patent source-offer decisions
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Turing-specific license, advisory, notice, patent, source-offer, and redistribution decisions for the selected baseline.
Native source-build recipes or binary-package exceptions, notice/advisory review, package minimization, and final package manifest.
Deterministic native bootstrap download verification policy, independent replay, provenance review, and redistribution decision.
Owner-reviewed generator manifest, owner-reviewed source-to-output provenance map, feature-correct clean regeneration, independent-host comparison, and release-boundary decisions.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-008 registry git path build-script and proc-macro side effects
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Selected baseline/profile, accepted side-effect policy, dynamic tracing, proc-macro provenance, and expansion-risk review.
Block-level unsafe ledger, SAFETY coverage, invariants, ownership, tests, fuzz/sanitizer/Miri or equivalent evidence, and release relevance classification.
Accepted FFI ABI contracts, ownership, conformance tests, sanitizer/fuzz/Miri or equivalent evidence, and release-boundary decisions.
Owner-selected option, source baseline, feature profile, target platform, dependency closures, in/out component lists, accepted APIs, replacement contracts, and maintenance boundaries.
Owner-reviewed option-by-option JavaScript impact decision, no-JIT proof, SpiderMonkey dependency analysis, Web IDL plan, GC/wrapper/realm model, DevTools impact, and Test262 strategy.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-013 compatibility corpus and WPT/Test262 evidence
evidence_axes.required_evidence: HTTPS harness execution, host-alias browser runs, raw logs, focused WPT subset, disabled/expected/timeout/crash/unsupported accounting, failure denominator, flakiness policy, and Test262 plan.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-014 fixed-hardware performance and memory baseline
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Runner-generated local corpus baseline, raw samples, hashes, owner-approved hardware/OS image, lifecycle disclosure, memory metrics, startup/input/frame/CPU/wakeup/energy measures, and equal-workload denominator.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-015 security model and sandbox implications
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed process authority map, sandbox boundary deltas, negative sandbox tests, identity preservation, native/media/GPU/runtime/update risk review, and compromised-process evidence.
Owner-approved patch ownership, upstream cadence policy, security response, merge burden, breakage handling, rollback plan, and primary/backup owners.

evidence_axes.axis: ADR9-EV-017 public claims requirements risks support impact
evidence_axes.required_evidence: Owner-selected option, document and registry diff, final unsupported behavior, residual risk, support language, and claim boundaries.
Accepted, rejected, or superseded ADR text with owner approvals, rejected options, time-bounded exceptions, and post-decision work packages.

option_axes.axis: Option A clean implementation informed by Servo
option_axes.required_evidence: Owner review preserves independent-engine claims while treating Servo only as external research input.
Owner review names accepted components, excluded components, source baseline, dependency closure, replacement contracts, support language, and residual risks.
Owner review defines contribution policy, patch ownership, review expectations, evidence-ingestion boundaries, and disclosure coordination.
Owner review rewrites charter, JavaScript runtime, dependency, security, support, release, maintenance, and public claim records before any Servo-derived claim.

option_axes.axis: Option E explicit Servo browser charter change
option_axes.required_evidence: Owner review rebuilds product identity, market strategy, requirements, risks, roadmap, support, governance, release, and security boundaries.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Named owner selects, rejects, or supersedes exactly one ADR-0009 direction with rationale.
Named owner selects source baseline, feature profile, target platforms, and rerun rules.
Every ADR9-EV item is owner-reviewed, explicitly rejected, or held by a time-bounded exception.
Every exception has owner approval, expiry, risk linkage, support-boundary update, and rollback path.
Owner and legal/community reviewers accept dependency, license, advisory, notice, patent, and source-offer impacts.
Owner and security reviewers accept process authority, sandbox, identity, update, and compromised-process implications.
Owner and quality/JavaScript reviewers accept compatibility denominator, WPT/Test262 strategy, and runtime implications.
Owner and performance reviewers accept fixed-hardware raw artifacts, statistics, lifecycle disclosure, and claim limits.
Owner and release/support reviewers accept upstream, patch, merge, support, update, and rollback obligations.

owner_review_axes.axis: public claim and registry diff review
owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner applies all required documents, requirements, risks, work packages, registries, support language, unsupported behavior, and residual-risk updates.

required_document_updates.required_evidence: Update Blueprint 17 or linked ADR record with accepted decision, rejected options, evidence, exceptions, and post-decision work.
Update PB-002 status, evidence, remaining blockers, allowed work, and source/provenance experiment language.
Close, supersede, or explicitly reject every ADR9-EV item with owner rationale.
Replace the evidence queue with the accepted operating path and remaining owner-only blockers.
Move from packet/matrix queue to accepted decision record or rejected/superseded queue.
Update root README, Start Here, and docs index status and onboarding language.
Update charter, parity, language/dependency, engine, JavaScript, security, performance, compatibility, release, roadmap, risks, DoD, and product requirements.
Update engine, JavaScript, security, performance, benchmark, release, technology stack, embedding, developer experience, API design, and competitive books.
Update requirements, risks, work packages, ownership, traceability, review rules, exceptions, and support statements.
Update SBOM, advisory, license, native, generated, unsafe, FFI, build-script, proc-macro, and source provenance ledgers.
Update benchmark manifests, browser pins, corpus, hardware, OS controls, artifacts, claim bundles, and performance claim boundaries.
Update support, unsupported behavior, vulnerability response, release, and public-claim language for the selected option.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim template is cited as an accepted ADR-0009 decision.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject source-strategy decision, keep PB-002 blocked, and keep decision_status no_source_strategy_decision.

rejection_rules.condition: Selected option, source baseline, feature profile, owner reviewer, or independent reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, or self-approved.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner-selected option, source baseline, and review claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Any ADR9-EV item has unclosed evidence and remains partial, missing, blocked, or not owner-reviewed without an approved expiring exception.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-002 readiness promotion and source adoption claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Source baseline, provenance, equivalence, archive, blob, legal, rerun, or feature-profile evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject Servo source strategy and source import claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Dependency, SBOM, advisory, license, notice, patent, source-offer, native-package, generated-output, unsafe, or FFI evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject dependency approval and component approval claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Independent clean-target build replay, target/cache isolation, or retained log bundle is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject build reproduction reuse for source-strategy decisions.

rejection_rules.condition: Component boundaries, replacement contracts, JavaScript-runtime impact, SpiderMonkey reachability, Web IDL plan, GC model, or Test262 strategy is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject component approval and JavaScript-runtime change claims.

rejection_rules.condition: HTTPS harness execution, host-alias browser runs, WPT/Test262 denominator, raw artifacts, or flakiness policy is missing.
Fixed-hardware runner-generated raw artifacts, lifecycle disclosure, memory metrics, statistics, or equal-workload denominator is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject performance, memory, energy, and Chrome-class claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Security model, sandbox negative tests, process authority, identity preservation, update risk review, or compromised-process evidence is missing.
Maintenance model, patch ownership, upstream cadence, security response, merge burden, rollback, or backup ownership evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject support commitment and release-code authorization claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Required document, requirement, risk, work-package, registry, support-language, unsupported-behavior, or residual-risk diff is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject public claim and readiness promotion claims.

rejection_rules.condition: A source-strategy exception lacks owner approval, expiry, risk linkage, support-boundary update, or rollback path.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject ADR acceptance and PB-002 status promotion.

rejection_rules.condition: Servo source, Servo-derived code, component approval, source import, release-code authorization, or implementation authority is inferred from planning evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the authority claim and keep PB-002 blocked.
